name: skill.md — Claude Code skill orchestration
applicability: 10-step pipeline with parallel agent dispatch, PreToolUse hook pattern, CLAUDE.md integration, detection→extraction→build→cluster→report pipeline. Directly comparable to SoNash skill architecture.
home_connection: SoNash skills use the same SKILL.md format. graphify's 10-step orchestration with agent parallelization is a mature reference for how complex multi-step skills should work.

name: skill-windows.md — Windows platform adaptation
applicability: Windows-specific path handling, python vs python3 detection, PowerShell compatibility. SoNash runs on Windows — this is directly applicable.
home_connection: SoNash runs on Windows with PowerShell. graphify's Windows-specific skill file shows how to handle platform differences in skill orchestration.

name: Platform skill files (5 variants)
applicability: Cross-platform skill distribution pattern: one core skill, platform-specific variants. Shows how to handle Agent vs Task tool, hook availability, sequential fallback.
home_connection: JASON-OS portability domain — if skills need to work across platforms, this pattern is a reference.

name: ARCHITECTURE.md — Pipeline architecture
applicability: Clean pipeline design: detect→extract→build→cluster→analyze→report→export. Each stage is a single function. Plain dicts + NetworkX. No shared state. Module responsibility table.
home_connection: SoNash skill architecture could adopt this one-function-per-module pattern for complex pipelines. Compare to our current skill SKILL.md approach.

name: SECURITY.md — Threat model
applicability: Comprehensive threat model for a tool that fetches URLs and generates HTML. SSRF, XSS, path traversal, prompt injection, symlink traversal, encoding crashes — all with specific mitigations.
home_connection: SoNash security patterns (SECURITY_CHECKLIST.md, CODE_PATTERNS.md) cover similar ground. graphify's threat model is more concise and threat-surface-organized.

name: worked/example — Reproducible demo
applicability: 7-file reproducible example showing expected pipeline behavior. Good model for how to include worked examples in tool repos.
home_connection: SoNash has no equivalent worked-example directory for skills. Could be a pattern to adopt.

name: worked/httpx — Real-world output example
applicability: Shows what the tool produces on a real codebase (httpx). 144 nodes, 330 edges, 6 communities. GRAPH_REPORT.md demonstrates god nodes, surprising connections, suggested questions.
home_connection: Shows the kind of structural analysis graphify produces. Complementary to repo-analysis dimensional scoring.

name: MCP server (serve.py)
applicability: MCP stdio server exposing graph as 7 queryable tools. Lightweight pattern: load graph once, serve tools. No network listener.
home_connection: SoNash has an MCP memory server. graphify's MCP pattern is simpler and could inform JASON-OS MCP server design.

name: Git hooks (hooks.py)
applicability: Marker-based git hook install/uninstall. Post-commit auto-rebuild (code only, no LLM). Post-checkout rebuild on branch switch. Shebang injection prevention.
home_connection: SoNash pre-commit/pre-push hooks are more complex. graphify's marker-based install/uninstall pattern is cleaner for third-party hook integration.

name: Watch mode (watch.py)
applicability: watchdog-based file watcher with debounce. Code changes → instant AST rebuild. Doc changes → notification only. Pragmatic split: deterministic vs LLM-required.
home_connection: No direct equivalent in SoNash. Interesting pattern for future live-reload features.

name: Extraction schema pattern (validate.py + LanguageConfig)
applicability: LanguageConfig dataclass pattern — one config object per language with type-specific node resolution, call detection, import handling. Generic walker + per-language config = 20 languages with no code duplication.
home_connection: SoNash analysis-schema.js uses Zod for schema validation. graphify's LanguageConfig pattern shows how to handle per-type variation with a shared walker.
